gotchas.title: Patterns match AST shape, not raw text
gotchas.summary: Pattern matching should be treated as syntax-structure matching; regex is a last resort, not the primary matching strategy.
gotchas.content: Prefer pattern rules for clarity, add kind for stricter scopes, use relations for context, and reserve regex as a last resort.

gotchas.title: Meta-variables capture whole AST nodes
gotchas.summary: Meta-variables do not support partial identifier substring matching; capture the full node and constrain it instead.
gotchas.content: Do not write partial meta-variable patterns like foo$BAR. Capture the whole node and add constraints if needed.

gotchas.title: Do not rename or match identifiers by broad text sweeps alone
gotchas.summary: Match the intended API context explicitly instead of sweeping every token that happens to share a name.
gotchas.content: Do not use broad identifier sweeps that rename every matching token by text alone. Match the intended API context explicitly.

gotchas.title: Use dump_ast when patterns do not match expected AST shapes
gotchas.summary: If an ast-grep pattern is failing, inspect the actual AST shape before attempting broader fallbacks.
gotchas.content: When the pattern is unclear or not matching, inspect the AST node kinds and structure with dump_ast instead of jumping to manual parsing or regex.

recipes.title: Prefer js-ast-grep for JS/TS-family source edits
recipes.summary: The default package shape for JS/TS-family source transforms should be js-ast-grep.
recipes.content: When the requested codemod is mainly JS/TS-family source edits, use a js-ast-grep package rather than broader workflow or shell-first shapes.

recipes.title: Use semantic_analysis: workspace when symbol origin matters
recipes.summary: Cross-file symbol resolution belongs in workflow semantic analysis instead of manual import/name inference.
recipes.content: Use semantic_analysis: workspace in js-ast-grep steps when cross-file definitions or references are required for safe matching.
